_readme: SIGNALS config — DAVID OWNS THIS FILE. Which metric speaks to attraction vs retention vs engagement, and where the flag thresholds sit, is reward domain knowledge.
Signals are FLAGS with a peer fact attached — never advice. They only ever say 'worth a look'.

thresholds: behind_percentile = a mapped polarised metric flags when its polarity-adjusted percentile is at/below this; save_percentile = a cost metric flags when the org pays at/above this percentile; prevalence_floor = a mapped practice flags when peer adoption is at/above this and the org doesn't do it; money_min_gbp = a £-model gap must reach this to flag.
cost_metrics: NEUTRAL spend metrics eligible for the 'save' lens (a fact about cost, never a market verdict).
position_lenses: polarised metrics -> the lens their 'behind' flag carries.
prevalence_lenses: practice questions -> the lens their 'most peers do this, you don't' flag carries.
Edits hot-reload (no restart needed). max_signals caps the briefing — ranked money > save > behind > prevalence, by size within each.
max_per_lens balances the briefing — at most this many flags per lens.
